title: Drying handmade sheets — restraint drying versus free drying
sources: Dard Hunter, Old Papermaking (1923, public domain) — sheet drying and shrinkage
notes: Comparison of the two drying paths: free-drying (sheets dry loose, cockle slightly) vs restraint-drying (sheets dried flat under boards, or exchange-dried between blotters, producing flatter sheets with different surface properties). Effect on paper for writing vs bookbinding vs watercolour. How to dry on a board edge and when to size before or after drying.
